title: viola aurea var. venosa
intro: Viola aurea var. venosa, also known as the golden violet or the veiny golden violet, is a variant of the Viola aurea species, a flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Viola aurea var. venosa include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and promote relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Viola aurea var. venosa include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil.
